article_meta.title: Ukraine expected to give up land, some arms under US peace plan, sources sayBy Tom Balmforth and Anastasiia MalenkoNovem

reasoning: The model’s summary maintains neutral language and does not mirror any loaded framing; Reuters’ article is itself relatively neutral. Minor issues include lack of clear attribution for claims sourced to unnamed officials and omission of some counterbalancing perspectives, but there is no adoption of emotive or partisan rhetoric.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (signaled, includes, meeting) without emotive phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Presents sourced claims (U.S. plan, ceding land/arms) without explicit attribution to anonymous sources or Axios in the summary.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: States the U.S. ‘has signaled’ and Ukraine ‘must accept’ without attribution qualifiers, though these reflect the article’s sourced reporting.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarmist language added.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer intentions beyond reported positions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes U.S. pressure on Kyiv more than balancing elements like White House decline to comment and Rubio’s framing.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits perspectives from European diplomats and Rubio’s statements that temper the ‘must accept’ framing.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone matches source; no extra negative emphasis.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official sources and positions evenly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Overall neutral restatement without adopting loaded rhetoric.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Summary misses several viewpoints (White House non-comment, Rubio’s stance, European diplomats’ critiques), and sources_represented lists only ‘Reuters.’
